message:
🌱 Add cert-manager: Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit  mission (#1224)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "cert-manager-3958-sane-defaults-for-certificate-revision-history-limit",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "cert-manager: Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit ",
+    "description": "Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit . Requested by 20+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current cert-manager deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your cert-manager version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nhelm list -n cert-manager 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working cert-manager installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review cert-manager configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant cert-manager configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get configmap -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cert-manager\n```\nyes. https://github.com/jetstack/cert-manager/pull/3773 introduced garbage collecting certificateRequest resources. But, the default revisionHistoryLimit is nil, which means revisions will be not be garbage collected, by default."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit ",
+        "description": "It's not an ideal solution, but *if* any of you use Kyverno, my colleague  has been writing a tutorial which explains how to use a ClusterPolicy to apply sensible defaults to Certificates. PTAL:\n * https://github.com/cert-manager/website/pull/1279\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n cert-manager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cert-manager\nkubectl get events -n cert-manager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Sane defaults for Certificate revision history limit \" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "It's not an ideal solution, but *if* any of you use Kyverno, my colleague  has been writing a tutorial which explains how to use a ClusterPolicy to apply sensible defaults to Certificates. PTAL:\n * https://github.com/cert-manager/website/pull/1279",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "cert-manager",
+      "graduated",
+      "security",
+      "feature"
+    ],

+    ],

+    ],

+    },

+  },
